column4: Reflection Question 1
column5: Reflection Question 2

column4: Has this course changed the way you think about research or evidence in clinical practice? What stood out to you the most?
column5: What research question or idea are you most interested in exploring further, and what inspired that interest?
column6: Which part of the research process did you find most relevant to your own goals or setting?

column4: Think of a critically ill patient you've cared for. What aspect of their emergency management felt most challenging, and how does this course change how you'd approach it?
column5: What is the biggest barrier to delivering good emergency and critical care in your setting? How have you or your team tried to work around it?

column2: Certificate in Community Engagement & Involvement for Surgical Research
column4: What key concepts or definitions did you learn (for example, CEI, participation, engagement, involvement)?
column5: How did the module shape your understanding of involving patients, carers, and communities in surgical research?

column4: Think of a patient you've seen with acute abdominal pain. What clinical signs helped guide your diagnosis, and was there anything that made it difficult?
column5: How does your facility currently manage acute appendicitis, and are there gaps in care that you would like to see improved?

column2: Laparoscopic Handling of the Small Bowel
column4: Have you had experience with laparoscopic bowel surgery? What did you find most technically challenging about handling the small bowel?
column5: What resources or training would help you feel more confident with laparoscopic small bowel procedures in your setting?

column4: Reflect on your first experience with trocar placement. What did you find most difficult, and what would you do differently now?
column5: How do you currently decide on trocar placement for different procedures, and how has this course influenced your thinking?

column4: Have you managed a patient with ectopic pregnancy? What was most challenging about their care, and what was the outcome?
column5: What barriers in your setting might delay diagnosis and treatment of ectopic pregnancy, and what practical steps could address them?

column4: Think of a patient you've seen with penetrating trauma to the chest or abdomen. What were your immediate priorities in their management?
column5: What resources does your facility have for managing penetrating trauma, and what gaps affect patient outcomes in your setting?

column4: Reflect on a laparoscopic cholecystectomy you've performed or observed. What aspect of the procedure felt most technically demanding?
column5: How do you ensure safe dissection in the hepatocystic triangle in your practice, and what do you do when the anatomy is unclear?

column4: Have you encountered a patient with a suspected musculoskeletal tumour in your practice? How did you approach their diagnosis and initial management?
column5: What challenges do patients with musculoskeletal tumours face in accessing specialised care in your region, and how do you navigate these?

column2: Hemorrhage Control in the Prehospital Setting
column4: Have you ever had to manage life-threatening bleeding before reaching a health facility? What actions did you take, and what resources did you wish you had?
column5: What training do first responders and healthcare workers in your setting receive for prehospital hemorrhage control, and what improvements would you recommend?

column4: What key concepts did you learn about trauma care, disaster response, and team roles?
column5: How did the module shape your understanding of working within a multidisciplinary trauma team?

column2: Intervention de Traumatologie et de Catastrophe en Equipe
column4: Quels concepts clés avez-vous appris concernant la prise en charge des traumatismes, l'intervention en cas de catastrophe et les rôles au sein d'une équipe ?
column5: En quoi ce module a-t-il influencé votre compréhension du travail au sein d'une équipe multidisciplinaire de prise en charge des traumatismes ?

column2: Complex Lower Extremity Trauma – Femoral Neck Fractures
column4: Think of a patient you've managed with a femoral neck fracture. What factors influenced your decision between fixation and arthroplasty?
column5: What challenges does your facility face in managing femoral neck fractures — whether related to equipment, timing, or postoperative care?

column2: Complex Lower Extremity Trauma – Acetabular Fractures
column4: Have you managed or referred a patient with an acetabular fracture? What was most complex about their case?
column5: What does access to specialised orthopaedic trauma care look like in your setting, and how does this affect outcomes for patients with acetabular fractures?

column2: Apprendre et Transmettre l'Anesthésie Locorégionale Echoguidée en toute Sécurité
column4: How do you currently teach or learn ultrasound-guided regional anaesthesia in your setting? What has worked well and what has been difficult?
column5: Think about a regional anaesthesia block you've performed or supervised. What was the most challenging part of ensuring both safety and effectiveness?

column4: Have you ever worked in a high-acuity or conflict-affected setting? What aspects of nursing care were hardest to maintain under those conditions?
column5: What principles from this course do you think are most important for nurses to apply when working in crisis or conflict environments?

column4: Which foundational surgical principle from this course do you feel is most often overlooked in your day-to-day practice, and why?
column5: How do you approach teaching surgical fundamentals to junior colleagues or students in your setting?

column4: Have you worked in a resource-limited or high-pressure environment where standard anaesthesia equipment or drugs were unavailable? How did you adapt?
column5: What do you think is the most critical skill for an anaesthesia provider working in a conflict or austere setting, and how well prepared do you feel?

column2: Key Concepts in the Perioperative Management of Spinal Cord Injuries
column4: Have you cared for a patient with a spinal cord injury in the perioperative period? What aspect of their management felt most complex?
column5: What challenges does your facility face in providing safe anaesthesia and perioperative care for patients with spinal cord injuries?

column4: Have you ever had to perform surgery under austere or resource-limited conditions? What was the hardest part about adapting your approach?
column5: Which principle of war surgery do you think is most applicable to everyday surgical practice in your setting, even outside of conflict?

column4: Think about a time you performed or assisted with a rapid sequence induction. What went smoothly, and what would you approach differently?
column5: What is the most common scenario in your practice where RSI is needed, and how confident are you in managing it if complications arise?

column4: Think of a case where a labour epidural was not working as expected. What did you do to troubleshoot it, and what was the outcome?
column5: What is the most common epidural complication you encounter in your practice, and how do you currently manage it?

column4: Think about a patient who needed scalp reconstruction after trauma or tumour removal. What factors most influenced your choice of technique?
column5: What reconstructive options are realistically available to you in your practice, and how do you adapt when your first-choice approach isn't possible?

column2: Anaesthetic Considerations for Vaginal Birth After Caesarean Delivery
column4: Have you provided anaesthesia for a patient attempting a vaginal birth after caesarean? What was your biggest concern going into that case?
column5: How do you currently counsel patients about the anaesthetic risks of VBAC in your setting, and what factors most influence your approach?

column4: Think of a time when you felt you truly understood what mattered most to a patient. What made that interaction different, and how did it affect their care?
column5: What is one thing you could change in your day-to-day practice to make your care feel more patient-centred, even with limited time or resources?

column2: Lumbar Plexus Block – Landmark Technique (PSOAS Compartment Block)
column4: Have you performed or observed a lumbar plexus block? What was the most challenging aspect of the landmark technique in practice?
column5: How does regional anaesthesia fit into your current approach to postoperative pain management for lower limb surgery, and what limits its use in your setting?

column4: Think about a patient whose perioperative journey stood out to you — something went unexpectedly well or unexpectedly wrong. What role did nursing care play in that outcome?
column5: Which part of the perioperative nursing role do you find most challenging in your setting, and what support would make the biggest difference?

column4: Think about a patient you've seen with a mild or moderate burn injury. How confident did you feel in their assessment and treatment, and what would you do differently now that you've completed this course?
column5: Burn injuries are largely preventable. What are the most common causes of burns in the patients you see, and is there anything your facility or community could realistically do to reduce them?

column2: Blood Sparing Techniques in Paediatric Anaesthesia
column4: Have you cared for a child who required blood transfusion during surgery? Reflecting on that case, were there opportunities to reduce blood loss that weren't taken?
column5: What blood conservation strategies are currently used in your facility for paediatric patients, and what barriers prevent wider use of these techniques?

column4: Have you used or observed a TAP block in clinical practice? How effective was it for postoperative pain management, and what challenges came up?
column5: How does access to ultrasound guidance in your setting affect your ability to offer regional anaesthesia techniques like the TAP block to your patients?

column4: Think about a caesarean delivery you've anaesthetised. What factors influenced your choice between spinal, epidural, or general anaesthesia?
column5: What is the most common anaesthetic challenge you face during caesarean deliveries in your setting, and how do you manage it?

column2: Fascia Iliaca Compartment Block: Landmark And Ultrasound Approach
column4: Have you performed or assisted with a fascia iliaca block? What differences did you notice between the landmark and ultrasound-guided approaches?
column5: How do you currently manage acute pain from hip or femoral fractures in your facility, and how might this block improve care for those patients?

column2: Burns: Wound Care and Pain Management
column4: Think of a burn patient you've cared for. What was the most difficult part of managing their wound care, and how did you address it?
column5: How do you currently approach pain management for burn patients in your setting, and what changes would improve their comfort and recovery?

column4: Have you encountered an unexpected intraoperative complication? How did you respond, and what would you do differently with the knowledge from this course?
column5: Which intraoperative problem do you feel least prepared to manage in your current practice, and what would help you feel more confident?

column4: Have you been involved in a free flap reconstruction case? What aspect of microsurgery did you find most technically demanding?
column5: What factors in your setting — equipment, team experience, postoperative monitoring — affect your ability to offer free flap reconstruction to patients?

column2: A Crash Course in Facial Fractures
column4: Think of a patient you've seen with facial trauma. How confident did you feel assessing and planning their management, and what would have helped you more?
column5: What is the most common type of facial fracture you encounter in your practice, and how does your facility currently manage it?
column7: Questions need to be added into the reflection forum.

column4: Have you used tranexamic acid in a bleeding emergency or surgical case? What was the outcome, and did anything about how you use it change after this course?
column5: Is tranexamic acid routinely available and used in your facility? If not, what barriers exist, and how might wider use improve patient outcomes?

column4: Avez-vous déjà utilisé l'acide tranexamique dans le cadre d'une urgence hémorragique ou d'une intervention chirurgicale ? Quel a été le résultat, et votre façon de l'utiliser a-t-elle évolué à l'issue de cette formation ?
column5: L'acide tranexamique est-il couramment disponible et utilisé dans votre établissement ? Si ce n'est pas le cas, quels sont les obstacles rencontrés, et en quoi une utilisation plus généralisée pourrait-elle améliorer les résultats cliniques des patients ?

column4: Think of a patient with a hand injury you've managed. What was most challenging about their reconstruction, and how satisfied were you with the functional outcome?
column5: What is the biggest gap in hand trauma care in your setting — is it surgical expertise, rehabilitation, or access to follow-up?
column7: Questions need to be added into the reflection forum.

column4: Reflect on your daily use of PPE in the clinical setting. Are there situations where you feel the right protection isn't available or isn't being used correctly?
column5: Have you ever felt unsafe due to inadequate PPE? How did you handle it, and what changes would you advocate for in your workplace?

column4: How reliant is your clinical practice on pulse oximetry for patient monitoring? Have you ever encountered a situation where the reading was misleading?
column5: Is pulse oximetry reliably available for all patients in your facility? If not, how do you monitor oxygenation, and what impact does this have on safety?

column2: Pulse Oximetry in the Context of COVID-19
column4: During the COVID-19 pandemic, did you notice changes in how pulse oximetry was used in your setting? What did you learn from those experiences?
column5: How has the pandemic changed your approach to monitoring and triaging patients with respiratory symptoms in your practice?

column2: Conversion of Labour Epidural Analgesia to Anaesthesia for Caesarean Delivery
column4: Have you had to convert a labour epidural to surgical anaesthesia for an emergency caesarean? How smoothly did it go, and what challenges did you face?
column5: What factors in your setting — staffing, equipment, communication — most affect your ability to quickly convert epidural analgesia to anaesthesia when needed?

column2: Perioperative Blood Management in the Pediatric Patient
column4: Have you managed significant intraoperative blood loss in a paediatric patient? What strategies did you use, and what would you do differently?
column5: What blood conservation and transfusion protocols exist for children in your facility, and are they consistently followed?

column4: Have you performed or assisted with a femoral nerve block? How did ultrasound guidance change the procedure compared to landmark techniques?
column5: How do you currently manage postoperative pain after femoral or knee surgery in your setting, and where does nerve block fit in?

column2: Perioperative Nursing E-Learning Foundational Programme (PeN Programme)
column4: Which module in this programme addressed a gap you've felt in your own perioperative nursing practice? How will you apply what you learned?
column5: What aspect of perioperative nursing do you find hardest to maintain in your facility, and how does this programme help you advocate for better standards?

column4: Have you given or received a telephone consultation that didn't go as planned? What was missing from that interaction, and how would you approach it differently?
column5: How do you ensure safety and accuracy when assessing a patient you cannot see? What strategies do you use in your phone consultations?

column4: Have you ever conducted or participated in a video consultation? What worked well, and what made it harder than an in-person visit?
column5: Which types of patients in your practice do you think would most benefit from video consultations, and what barriers prevent you from offering them?

column4: Think of a time when a handover — at shift change, between departments, or at discharge — didn't go smoothly. What happened, and what was the impact on the patient?
column5: What handover practices in your workplace feel most reliable, and which feel most at risk of leading to a missed detail?

column4: Have you been involved in surgery for rectal cancer? What was the most technically challenging aspect of the procedure?
column5: How does access to colorectal oncology services — staging, multidisciplinary teams, and follow-up — affect outcomes for your patients with rectal cancer?

column4: Have you managed or referred a patient with pancreatic cancer? What was most complex about their surgical planning and care?
column5: What barriers do patients in your setting face in accessing timely diagnosis and surgical treatment for pancreatic cancer?

column4: Think of a patient you've cared for with colon cancer. What factors most influenced the surgical plan and outcome?
column5: How does your facility approach colon cancer surgery — are there multidisciplinary team discussions, and what happens when specialist input isn't available?

column4: Have you cared for a patient undergoing breast cancer surgery? How was the decision made between breast-conserving surgery and mastectomy?
column5: What challenges do patients with breast cancer face in your setting — in terms of diagnosis, access to surgery, or reconstruction options?

column4: Have you encountered a patient with a vascular malformation? How was the diagnosis made, and what treatment options were available?
column5: What is the biggest challenge in managing vascular malformations in your setting — diagnosis, referral pathways, or access to treatment?

column4: Have you cared for a patient with a pressure sore requiring reconstruction? What factors made their healing difficult?
column5: What systems exist in your facility to prevent pressure sores, and what happens when a patient develops one despite preventive measures?

column2: Adjuvant Medications for Peripheral Nerve Block
column4: Have you used adjuvant medications to extend or improve the quality of a peripheral nerve block? What was your experience, and what influenced your choice?
column5: How do you decide which adjuvants to use for regional anaesthesia in your practice, and what limitations affect your options?

column2: Anaesthesia for Craniotomy and Brain Tumour Resection
column4: Have you provided anaesthesia for a craniotomy or brain tumour resection? What aspect of the case required the most careful planning?
column5: What intraoperative challenges do you most commonly face during neurosurgical cases in your setting, and how do you manage them?

column2: Nerve Stimulation for Peripheral Nerve Blockade
column4: Have you used nerve stimulation for peripheral nerve blocks? How did the technique compare to landmark or ultrasound-guided approaches in your experience?
column5: In settings without reliable ultrasound access, how do you ensure the safety and accuracy of peripheral nerve blocks?

column2: Obstetrics in the Setting of Remote Retrieval Medicine
column4: Have you ever managed an obstetric emergency in a remote or resource-limited setting? What was your biggest challenge in accessing help or specialist care?
column5: What preparation would make you feel more confident managing an obstetric emergency before specialist retrieval arrives?

column2: Anaesthesia for Posterior Cranial Fossa Surgery
column4: Have you provided anaesthesia for posterior fossa surgery? What positioning or airway challenges did you encounter, and how did you manage them?
column5: What special considerations do you factor into your anaesthetic plan for patients undergoing posterior cranial fossa surgery in your setting?

column4: Have you ever suspected or managed a case of local anaesthetic systemic toxicity? What were the early signs, and how quickly were you able to respond?
column5: Is your facility prepared to manage LAST — do you have lipid emulsion available and does your team know the protocol? What would you do if not?

column4: Think of a time when capnography gave you information that changed your clinical decision. What did you learn from that experience?
column5: Is capnography reliably available in your practice? If not, how do you monitor ventilation and detect problems like oesophageal intubation?

column4: Have you ever had a blood pressure reading that didn't match the clinical picture? How did you investigate, and what did you find?
column5: How does your practice approach blood pressure monitoring for critically ill patients — when do you move from non-invasive to invasive monitoring, and what determines that decision?

column2: Principles of Regional Anaesthesia in Children
column4: Have you performed regional anaesthesia in a paediatric patient? What differences did you notice compared to adults in terms of technique or dosing?
column5: How do you approach parental consent and a child's own understanding when planning regional anaesthesia in your practice?

column2: Practical Considerations to Access the Epidural Space
column4: Think of a challenging epidural insertion you've performed or observed. What made it difficult, and how did you work through it?
column5: What factors in your setting — patient population, equipment, positioning — most affect your success rate with epidural placement?

column2: Speaking Up in the Operating Room: Escalating Concerns
column4: Have you ever felt uncomfortable speaking up about a safety concern in the operating room? What made it hard, and what happened as a result?
column5: What does the culture in your operating room feel like when it comes to raising concerns? What would make it easier for everyone to speak up, regardless of seniority?

column4: How does your facility approach perioperative medical optimisation? Are there patients you feel aren't being adequately prepared for surgery?
column5: What is the most common perioperative medical complication you encounter in your practice, and how do you currently manage it?

column2: Oral Health in Comprehensive Cleft Care: Oral Health Professionals
column4: Have you been part of a multidisciplinary cleft care team? How well was oral health integrated into the overall management plan?
column5: What barriers do patients with cleft lip and palate face in accessing dental and oral health care in your setting?

column4: Have you provided anaesthesia for a free flap case? What intraoperative priorities — perfusion, temperature, fluid management — felt most critical?
column5: How do you manage communication between the surgical and anaesthesia team during free flap surgery to ensure flap survival?

column2: Fascia Iliaca Compartment Block: An Update
column4: How has your understanding of the fascia iliaca block changed since this update, and how will it affect how you perform or teach the block?
column5: In what clinical situations do you find the fascia iliaca block most useful, and are there cases where you've avoided it — if so, why?

column4: Think of a patient with acute burns you've managed. What was your biggest challenge in the first 24 hours of their care?
column5: How does your facility handle burn resuscitation and wound assessment? What resources or protocols are missing that would improve care?

column4: Have you been involved in a head and neck reconstruction case? What factors most influenced the choice of reconstructive technique?
column5: What challenges do patients undergoing head and neck reconstruction face in your setting — functional, aesthetic, or psychosocial?

column4: Have you cared for a patient with facial paralysis? How did their condition affect them beyond the physical — emotionally, socially, professionally?
column5: What treatment options for facial paralysis are realistically available in your setting, and how do you counsel patients when ideal options aren't accessible?

column4: Have you been involved in or referred a patient for breast reconstruction after mastectomy? How was the timing and type of reconstruction decided?
column5: What do women in your setting typically know about breast reconstruction options before surgery, and how do you approach that conversation?

column4: Think of a patient who required nasal reconstruction. What was the most complex aspect of planning and executing the repair?
column5: How do you balance aesthetic and functional outcomes in nasal reconstruction, especially when resources are limited?

column4: Have you cared for a child with cleft lip and/or palate, or been part of a repair team? What aspect of their management was most complex?
column5: What does the multidisciplinary care pathway for cleft patients look like in your setting, and where are the biggest gaps?

column4: Have you reconstructed a defect after skin cancer excision? What guided your choice of technique, and how satisfied were you with the result?
column5: What challenges do patients in your setting face in getting timely skin cancer diagnosis and surgical treatment?

column4: Have you performed or observed ear reconstruction? What was the most technically challenging aspect of the procedure?
column5: How do you approach the psychological and social impact of ear deformity with your patients, particularly in children?

column4: Think of a patient who required lip or cheek reconstruction after trauma or tumour removal. What were the functional and aesthetic goals, and how well were they achieved?
column5: What reconstructive options are available to you for lip and cheek defects, and how do you adapt when preferred techniques aren't possible?

column4: Have you performed a Z-plasty in your practice? What were the indications, and how did you determine the angle and limb lengths?
column5: How do you explain Z-plasty to patients or families before the procedure — what outcomes do you emphasise, and how do you manage expectations?

column2: BIG T Trauma Series – ED Thoracotomy
column4: Have you ever been present for or performed an emergency department thoracotomy? What was the indication, and what was the outcome?
column5: How does your team decide when an ED thoracotomy is indicated versus futile, and how is that decision communicated in the moment?

column2: BIG T Trauma Series – Brain Death
column4: Have you ever been involved in the assessment of brain death? What was the most challenging part — clinical, ethical, or communicating with the family?
column5: How does your facility approach organ donation after brain death, and what barriers affect this process in your setting?

column2: Trauma Surgery Video Atlas – Trauma Laparotomy
column4: Have you performed an exploratory or damage control laparotomy for trauma? What was the most critical decision you had to make intraoperatively?
column5: What aspects of trauma laparotomy technique do you feel least confident with, and how do you seek support or guidance in those situations?

column4: Have you ever witnessed a break in aseptic technique during a procedure? How was it identified, and how was it handled?
column5: What is the biggest challenge to maintaining strict aseptic technique in your workplace — environment, staffing, training, or culture?

column4: Think of a patient whose preoperative assessment revealed something unexpected that changed the surgical plan. What did you learn from that case?
column5: How thorough is your facility's preoperative assessment process? What is commonly missed, and what would improve it?

column4: Have you cared for a patient who deteriorated unexpectedly in the postoperative period? What early signs, if any, were present before the deterioration?
column5: What monitoring is routinely available for postoperative patients in your setting, and what gaps concern you most?

column4: Have you managed a complication related to a central venous access device — infection, thrombosis, or mechanical issue? How was it identified and handled?
column5: What protocols does your facility use to prevent CVAD-associated infections, and how consistently are they followed?

column4: Think about a time you placed or assisted with placing a CVAD. What was the most technically challenging part, and how did you ensure safety?
column5: How does your team decide which type of central venous access is most appropriate for a given patient and clinical situation?

column4: What urological conditions do you most commonly encounter in your practice, and which ones feel hardest to manage without specialist support?
column5: Have you had to manage a urological emergency without immediate access to a specialist? What did you do, and what would have helped you?

column4: Have you been involved in a chest wall reconstruction case? What was the indication, and what factors influenced the reconstructive plan?
column5: What are the biggest challenges in achieving both structural stability and acceptable aesthetics in chest wall reconstruction in your setting?

column4: Have you managed a patient requiring burn reconstruction for contractures or scarring? What was the most difficult aspect of planning or executing the procedure?
column5: How long after the initial burn injury do your patients typically wait before accessing reconstructive surgery, and how does this affect outcomes?

column4: Think of a mass casualty or multi-patient scenario you've been involved in. How was triage conducted, and what would you do differently?
column5: What triage system does your facility use, and how confident are you that your team can implement it effectively under pressure?

column4: Have you managed a patient with significant head and neck trauma? What was your biggest concern in their initial assessment?
column5: What are the most common causes of head and neck trauma in your setting, and does your facility have clear protocols for managing the airway in these patients?

column4: Have you ever cared for a patient with a chemical or radiation injury? What challenges did you face in their management?
column5: How prepared is your facility to identify and safely manage chemical or radiation exposure — in terms of protocols, equipment, and staff training?

column4: Think of a patient with moderate to severe burns you've cared for. What aspect of their management was most challenging in the first 48 hours?
column5: How does your facility's capacity to manage burns affect how you prioritise and plan care for these patients?

column4: Have you cared for a patient with blast injuries? What made their management different from other trauma cases?
column5: What does your facility's preparation look like for managing blast or mass casualty trauma — and where are the biggest gaps?

column4: Have you encountered a healthcare-associated infection in your unit that could have been prevented? What do you think led to it?
column5: What infection control practices are hardest to maintain consistently in your facility, and what would make them easier to follow?

column4: Have you ever used an emergency checklist during a crisis in the operating room? Did it help, and was it easy to access when you needed it?
column5: What is your honest assessment of checklist use in your anaesthesia practice — are they used consistently, and if not, what gets in the way?

column4: Have you ever made or witnessed a drug error in anaesthesia practice? What contributed to it, and what happened as a result?
column5: What systems exist in your workplace to prevent drug errors, and what additional safeguards do you think would make the biggest difference?

column4: Have you managed a difficult or failed airway? Walk through how it happened — what helped, what didn't, and what you'd do differently?
column5: How familiar is your team with the current difficult airway algorithm, and does your facility have the necessary equipment for each step?

column4: When do you choose a vertical mattress suture over other techniques in your practice, and how has your approach to wound closure evolved?
column5: Think of a wound where you weren't happy with the final closure. What would you do differently using what you've learned?

column4: When do you use a horizontal mattress suture, and what are the most common mistakes you've seen in its application?
column5: How do you teach suturing technique to junior colleagues or students in your setting, and what do they find hardest to master?

column4: How confident are you with one-handed knot-tying in both hands, and what situations in your practice require this skill most often?
column5: What is the most common knot-tying mistake you see in your trainees, and how do you help them correct it?

column4: How do you decide when to use a two-handed tie versus other techniques during surgery, and does the depth or access of the field influence your choice?
column5: How much deliberate practice in knot-tying do you think is realistic for trainees in your setting, and what methods work best?

column2: Basic Communication Skills for Frontline Healthcare Workers
column4: Think of a difficult conversation with a patient or family member that didn't go well. What would you do differently using the communication skills from this course?
column5: What aspect of clinical communication do you find hardest in your day-to-day practice — breaking bad news, gaining informed consent, or something else?

column2: Hand Washing, Asepsis, Disinfection and Sterilization
column4: How consistently is hand hygiene practised in your facility, and what are the most common moments when it gets skipped?
column5: Have you ever seen a patient develop a preventable infection linked to a failure in hand hygiene or sterilisation? What did that experience teach you?

column4: Think of a wound that took longer to heal than expected. What factors contributed to the delay, and what would you do differently?
column5: What wound management resources are reliably available in your setting, and how do you adapt when preferred dressings or supplies aren't available?

column4: Which suture technique from this course do you find most applicable to your everyday practice, and why?
column5: What is the most common wound complication — dehiscence, infection, or poor scarring — that you see in your patients, and how does suture technique play a role?

column4: Have you performed or assisted with a difficult caesarean — previous scarring, morbid adhesions, or abnormal placentation? What was the most challenging part?
column5: How does your team prepare for and manage the risk of a difficult caesarean in your setting, and what support systems are in place?

column4: Think about the first caesarean section you performed or assisted with. What felt most unfamiliar, and how has your confidence grown since?
column5: How do you ensure safety for both mother and baby during a caesarean in your setting, especially when things don't go as planned?

column4: Think of a patient with burns who came to your facility. What was your first priority, and how did you assess the severity of their injury?
column5: What are the most common mistakes in early burn management that you've seen in your practice, and how do you try to prevent them?

column2: Introduction to C-Arm Imaging and Radiation Safety in the Operating Room
column4: How familiar were you with radiation safety practices before this course, and what is one thing you will change in your daily use of the C-arm?
column5: How does your team currently manage radiation exposure in the operating room — do people wear dosimeters, and is protective equipment consistently used?

column4: How is the WHO Surgical Safety Checklist used in your operating room — is it performed as intended, or has it become routine without real engagement?
column5: Have you ever seen the checklist catch something that could have led to harm? What happened, and how did it affect your team's attitude toward it?

column4: Think of a system in your workplace that frequently fails or is inconsistently followed. What do you think is the root cause, and how might you redesign it?
column5: Have you ever tried to introduce a new process or protocol in your setting? What made it succeed or fail?

column2: Safety Culture – Fundamentals of Patient Safety 2024
column4: How would you describe the safety culture in your workplace — does your team feel comfortable reporting errors and near-misses without fear?
column5: What is one specific thing you could do as an individual to help strengthen safety culture in your team, regardless of your role or seniority?

column2: Medication Error Prevention – Fundamentals of Patient Safety 2024
column4: Have you ever made or nearly made a medication error? What contributed to it, and what safeguards caught or could have caught it?
column5: What changes to prescribing, dispensing, or administration processes in your facility would most reduce the risk of medication errors?

column2: Interprofessional Communication – Fundamentals of Patient Safety 2024
column4: Think of a situation where poor communication between professions — doctors, nurses, pharmacists — affected patient care. What went wrong, and what would have helped?
column5: How well do different professional groups in your workplace communicate and collaborate, and what is one step you could take to improve it?

column2: Error Analysis and Prevention – Fundamentals of Patient Safety 2024
column4: Have you ever been involved in a root cause analysis or incident review? What did the process reveal that wasn't immediately obvious?
column5: How does your facility respond when errors occur — is the focus on learning and system improvement, or on blame? How does that affect reporting?

column4: Have you used epidemiological data in your clinical or public health practice? What did it reveal that changed how you approached a problem?
column5: How are patient safety data and outcomes collected and used in your facility — and what would help you use them more effectively to improve care?

column2: Communicating Effectively – Fundamentals of Patient Safety 2024
column4: Think of a clinical miscommunication that led to — or nearly led to — patient harm. What would effective communication have looked like in that situation?
column5: What is one communication habit or tool from this course that you want to start using in your practice, and what might get in the way?

column2: C-Arm Intraoperative Imaging for Ankle Fractures
column4: Have you used C-arm imaging for ankle fracture fixation? What did you find most challenging about obtaining and interpreting good intraoperative views?
column5: How does access to intraoperative imaging in your setting affect the quality and confidence of your fracture reductions?

column4: Have you ever encountered a surgical instrument failure or malfunction during a procedure? What happened, and how was it managed?
column5: What processes exist in your facility for instrument testing and inspection before procedures, and how consistently are they followed?

column4: Have you managed a patient with oesophageal perforation? What was the most time-critical decision in their care?
column5: How does your facility approach oesophageal perforation — are there clear pathways for surgical versus conservative management, and what determines the choice?

column4: Have you managed obstetric trauma — from a road traffic accident, assault, or other cause? What aspect of care was most challenging?
column5: How does your facility balance the needs of a pregnant trauma patient when maternal and fetal priorities may conflict?

column4: Have you been involved in the management of cardiac trauma? What was the most critical step in the initial assessment?
column5: What resources does your facility have for managing cardiac trauma — and what would you do if a patient presented with suspected cardiac injury and those resources weren't available?

column4: Have you managed a patient with acute mesenteric ischaemia? What made the diagnosis difficult, and how quickly were you able to act?
column5: What factors in your setting — imaging availability, vascular surgical support, ICU capacity — most affect outcomes for patients with mesenteric ischaemia?

column2: Informed Consent & Team Communication During Cesarean Section
column4: How do you currently obtain informed consent for caesarean section in your setting — is there enough time, privacy, and support for the patient to make a truly informed decision?
column5: Think of a case where communication within the team during a caesarean section broke down. What caused it, and what would have made it better?

column4: Think of a patient outcome — good or bad — where nursing care made a decisive difference. What did nurses do (or not do) that shaped that outcome?
column5: What do you think is the biggest gap between the nursing care your patients need and what is consistently delivered in your facility?

column4: How do you assess pain in your patients — do you feel confident that your assessment tools are capturing what patients are actually experiencing?
column5: Have you cared for a patient with chronic pain who wasn't getting adequate relief? What barriers existed, and what would have helped?

column4: Which surgical skill covered in this course do you feel most needs reinforcement in your own practice, and what would help you improve it?
column5: How do you build and maintain surgical skills in your setting — through formal training, supervised practice, or another approach?

column4: Have you ever been involved in a case of accidental intra-arterial injection? How was it recognised, and how was it managed?
column5: What steps do you take in your practice to prevent accidental intra-arterial injections, and are these consistently followed by your team?

column2: Implications of Anaesthesia on Cancer Surgery
column4: Has the type of anaesthesia — regional versus general — influenced decisions in cancer surgery in your practice? How was that conversation handled?
column5: What do you think is the most underappreciated way in which anaesthesia can affect cancer outcomes, and how has this course changed your thinking?

column2: Brainstem Reflexes During Neurosurgery: An Overview
column4: Have you monitored or been present during neurosurgery where brainstem reflexes were relevant? What was your role, and what did you observe?
column5: How does your team communicate intraoperatively about neurophysiological changes — and what happens when an unexpected finding appears?

column2: Prevention, Diagnosis, and Treatment of Fracture-Related Infections
column4: Have you managed a patient with a fracture-related infection? What made the diagnosis challenging, and what treatment approach did you use?
column5: What risk factors for fracture-related infection are most common in your patient population, and what do you do to reduce them?

column4: Think of a time when a distraction in the operating room — noise, interruptions, unnecessary conversation — affected your focus. What was the impact?
column5: What is the most common source of distraction in your operating room, and what practical steps could reduce it without disrupting team communication?

column2: Cancer in the Context of Universal Health Coverage
column4: How does your patient population access cancer screening, diagnosis, and treatment — and where do the biggest gaps in coverage exist?
column5: Think of a patient with cancer who faced barriers to treatment due to cost, geography, or lack of services. What did their journey look like, and what could have been different?

column4: Have you managed a wound complication after caesarean section — infection, dehiscence, or poor healing? What factors contributed, and how was it treated?
column5: How do you counsel patients before caesarean section about wound care and what to watch for after discharge?

column4: How do you currently assess a patient's functional capacity before surgery? What tools or questions do you find most useful in practice?
column5: Have you ever cancelled or delayed a procedure because of concerns identified during preoperative functional assessment? What happened, and was the outcome better as a result?

column2: Animal Bite and Insect Sting Injury
column4: Have you managed a patient with a serious animal bite or insect sting? What was the most critical decision in their initial management?
column5: What is the most common animal bite or sting injury you see in your setting, and does your facility have the resources to manage severe reactions or infections?

column4: Think about a caesarean section that was more technically challenging than expected. What made it difficult, and how did you adapt your approach?
column5: How do you make decisions about incision type and technique during caesarean section, and how does the clinical situation — urgency, previous scars, presentation — affect those choices?

column4: Have you performed laparoscopic appendectomy for uncomplicated appendicitis? How confident do you feel with the procedure, and what do you find most technically demanding?
column5: How does your facility decide between laparoscopic and open appendectomy — and what factors in your setting influence that decision?

column4: Have you managed complicated appendicitis laparoscopically — with perforation, abscess, or peritonitis? How did you approach the case, and when would you convert to open?
column5: What are the biggest challenges in managing complicated appendicitis in your setting, and how do outcomes differ from uncomplicated cases?

column4: Have you used or observed the Bangla approach to laparoscopic cholecystectomy? How does it compare to your standard technique in terms of safety and ease?
column5: What factors in your setting — equipment, training, patient anatomy — most affect the safety of laparoscopic cholecystectomy?

column4: Have you performed or assisted with a laparoscopic pyloromyotomy? What was the most technically demanding part of the procedure?
column5: How does your facility determine whether to approach pyloric stenosis laparoscopically or through open surgery, and what influences that decision?

column4: Have you performed laparoscopic inguinal hernia repair? What do you find most technically challenging, and how does it compare to open repair in your practice?
column5: How do you decide between TAPP and TEP approaches in your patients, and what factors — anatomy, previous surgery, resources — influence that decision?

column4: Have you managed a child with intussusception? How was the decision made between hydrostatic reduction and surgical intervention?
column5: What resources does your facility have for managing intussusception in children, and what happens when imaging or non-operative reduction isn't available?

column4: Have you performed or observed laparoscopic orchiopexy? What is the most important factor in deciding the surgical approach for an undescended testis?
column5: How does your setting handle the diagnosis and referral of children with undescended testes, and are there delays that affect outcomes?

column4: Have you managed a patient with both brain and spinal cord injuries? How did you prioritise and coordinate their care?
column5: What is the biggest challenge in your setting when managing polytrauma patients with neurological injuries — imaging, surgical capacity, or ICU support?

column2: Perioperative Management of Patients Undergoing Orthopaedic Oncological Surgeries
column4: Have you been involved in the perioperative care of a patient undergoing orthopaedic tumour surgery? What made the anaesthetic or surgical management particularly complex?
column5: What multidisciplinary support is available for orthopaedic oncology patients in your setting, and what gaps affect their care?

column4: Have you provided anaesthesia for a patient with significant burns? What was the most challenging aspect of airway or haemodynamic management?
column5: How does your facility approach the anaesthetic management of burn patients requiring multiple surgical procedures — what protocols or adaptations have you found most useful?

column4: Think of a patient with abdominal trauma you've managed. How was the decision made between operative and non-operative management?
column5: What imaging and surgical resources does your facility have for abdominal trauma, and how do gaps in these resources affect patient outcomes?

column4: Think of a patient with significant chest trauma. What was your first clinical priority, and how did you assess for life-threatening injuries?
column5: How does your facility manage tension pneumothorax, haemothorax, or flail chest — what works well, and where are the gaps?

column4: Have you provided anaesthesia in a resource-limited, austere, or conflict-affected environment? What adaptations did you have to make?
column5: Which anaesthetic techniques and skills do you think are most essential when working in settings where standard equipment, drugs, or infrastructure may not be available?

column4: Have you ever responded to a disaster or mass casualty event as part of a healthcare team? What was the biggest challenge in coordinating care?
column5: How prepared do you feel — personally and as part of your team — to respond to a sudden disaster in your area? What training or resources would make the biggest difference?

column4: Think of a trauma case where the operative technique you used was different from what you would have chosen in an elective setting. What drove that decision?
column5: Which operative technique for trauma surgery do you feel least confident with in your current practice, and how do you seek guidance when you face it?

column4: Have you managed a patient with complex orthopaedic trauma — multiple fractures, open injuries, or vascular compromise? What was your first priority?
column5: How does your facility handle orthopaedic trauma cases requiring specialised implants or fixation — are these reliably available, and what happens when they're not?

column4: Think of a trauma patient you managed perioperatively. What aspect of their care — haemorrhage control, warming, coagulopathy management — was hardest to address?
column5: How does your team approach damage control resuscitation and surgery for severely injured patients in your setting?

column4: Have you used FAST or extended FAST ultrasound in a trauma assessment? What did it change about your clinical decision-making?
column5: Is point-of-care ultrasound reliably available in your emergency or trauma setting? If not, how does that affect how you manage undifferentiated shock?

column2: A Comprehensive Approach to Financing Global Surgical Care
column4: Have you ever had a patient who couldn't access surgery because of cost? What happened to them, and how did it affect you as a clinician?
column5: What do you think is the most important financial barrier to surgical access in your setting, and what advocacy or policy change would make the biggest difference?

column4: Have you ever adapted a piece of equipment or developed a workaround solution because of resource constraints? What was the problem, and what did you create or adapt?
column5: What do you think is the most important quality for a healthcare innovator in a low-resource setting — creativity, persistence, collaboration, or something else?

column4: Have you ever adapted a piece of equipment or developed a workaround solution because of resource constraints? What was the problem, and what did you create or adapt?
column5: What do you think is the most important quality for a healthcare innovator in a low-resource setting — creativity, persistence, collaboration, or something else?
